data.title: U.S. Prepares Military Response Amid Iran Peace Talks, Says Trump
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that if peace negotiations with Iran fail, American warships are being equipped with advanced ammunition to resume strikes. According to Odaily, Trump's comments came shortly before U.S. Vice President Vance boarded Air Force Two en route to Islamabad for peace talks with Witkoff and Kushner.

In a phone interview, Trump expressed uncertainty about the success of the negotiations, saying, "We will know in about 24 hours. We are rearming our ships with the finest ammunition and the most advanced weapons, even better than what we used before, which had already defeated them. If no agreement is reached, we will use them very effectively."

Trump further commented on the unpredictability of the situation, stating, "We are dealing with people whose honesty is uncertain. They claim to abandon all nuclear weapons in front of us, but then tell the media they want to enrich uranium. We will find out the truth."

data.title: Trump: The outcome of talks with Iran will be clear within 24 hours
data.description: In an interview with the New York Post on the 10th, US President Trump said the outcome of talks with Iran would be clear within 24 hours. (Xinhua)

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 2,700 BTC and 30,000 ETH from Coinbase
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock withdrew 2,700 BTC ($196.87 million) and 30,000 ETH ($67.42 million) from Coinbase.

data.title: Circle minted 10.5 billion USDC on the Solana chain in the past month.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, Circle has just minted an additional 250 million USDC, bringing the total number of USDC minted on the Solana chain in the past month to 10.5 billion.

data.title: If peace talks with Iran fail, Trump is prepared to use force.
data.description: According to the New York Post: Trump is prepared to use force if peace talks with Iran fail. For weeks, U.S. allies, military analysts, and skeptics of the president's strategy have been asking: How was Iran allowed to seize and maintain control of the Strait of Hormuz? And, given the strength of the U.S. military, why doesn't the Trump administration use force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz? These questions will be particularly prominent during the peace talks that begin Saturday in Islamabad, as control of the waterway remains Iran's strongest bargaining chip. This will also test Trump's focus on voters' economic sensitivities and his caution regarding potential threats to the safety of American personnel. Former Marine Corps area military planner Mark Kancien said, "I'm surprised by the Navy's reluctance to seize the Strait itself. This isn't the Navy's most glorious moment. When the Navy finally needed to save the world economy, it completely failed." Former U.S. military officials and experts firmly believe that Iran's control of the Strait is not due to U.S. military incompetence. Former Central Command Commander Frank McKenzie stated earlier this month that the U.S. Navy has the capability to open and keep the Strait open if needed. Nevertheless, taking sustained military action for this is fraught with practical and political challenges. Former Central Command Deputy Commander Robert Howard stated that the risk to the Navy may be small, but the political risk to the president could be significant. (Jin Shi)

data.title: White House official: Trump is optimistic about reaching a deal with Iran
data.description: According to Fox News: White House officials say Trump is optimistic about reaching a deal with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: We are prepared militarily if Iran does not cooperate in negotiations.
data.description: Trump stated that if peace talks fail, US warships are being replenished with "the best ammunition" to resume strikes against Iran. Trump made these remarks shortly after Vice President Vance boarded Air Force Two bound for Islamabad, where he will meet with Witkov and Kushner for peace talks. When asked in a phone interview if he believed the talks would succeed, Trump said, "We'll know in about 24 hours. We'll know very soon." "We're resetting. We're loading the warships with the best ammunition and the most advanced, best-performing weapons—even better than the ones we used before, and we've already taken them down." "If we don't reach an agreement, we'll use them, and we'll use them very effectively." Trump said, "You're dealing with people we don't know if they're telling the truth. They come to our faces and say they'll give up all nuclear weapons, and that's it. Then they go out to the media and say, 'No, we want to enrich uranium.' So we'll figure it out." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Sources: Technical delegations from the United States and Iran have arrived in Pakistan.
data.description: Sources say that technical delegations from the United States and Iran arrived in Pakistan on Friday morning local time. The Iranian delegation will participate in negotiations on Saturday. Details of the Iranian delegation's arrival in Pakistan have been kept confidential, despite some comments made regarding the Lebanon situation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Quantum Threats Are Real but Not Immediate, Experts Say
data.description: Quantum computing poses a genuine threat to current cryptographic systems, but it is not an immediate concern. According to PANews, understanding which cryptographic components will be affected and how the industry plans to transition is crucial. Experts emphasize the importance of identifying vulnerable areas and preparing for a gradual migration to quantum-resistant technologies. The focus should be on strategic planning and readiness to ensure security in the face of future quantum advancements.

data.title: World's WLD Daily Unlock Rate to Decrease by 43% in 2026
data.description: World has announced that the daily unlock rate for WLD will automatically decrease by approximately 43% starting July 24, 2026. According to NS3.AI, this adjustment will reduce the total daily unlock amount from around 5.1 million WLD to about 2.9 million WLD. Currently, 4.9 billion WLD, representing 49% of the total supply, have already been unlocked, with approximately 3.3 billion WLD in circulation.

data.title: Hermès Expands Leather-Goods Production Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description: Hermès has inaugurated its 25th leather-goods manufacturing facility in France, continuing its strategy to boost production despite a slowdown in luxury demand. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's commitment to growth even as geopolitical tensions, such as the Iran conflict, introduce new uncertainties to the market. Hermès remains focused on expanding its operations, aiming to meet long-term goals despite current challenges in the luxury sector.

data.title: World Token Supply Dynamics and Unlocking Schedule Until 2026
data.description:
As of April 10, 2026, the total supply of WLD tokens is set at 10 billion, with 4.9 billion tokens, or 49%, already unlocked, and approximately 3.3 billion in circulation. According to BlockBeats, when WLD was launched on July 24, 2023, 500 million tokens were unlocked from the World Community allocation. The remaining 9.5 billion tokens are subject to a continuous daily linear unlocking mechanism, which will be completed 15 years after the launch.

Starting July 24, 2026, the unlocking speed for various tokens will automatically decrease due to the locking mechanism:

- Community Tokens: The unlocking speed will be reduced by 50%, from 3.2 million tokens per day to 1.6 million, as per an immutable on-chain contract.

- TFH Investor and Team Tokens: The unlocking speed will decrease by 32%, from 1.9 million tokens per day to 1.3 million.

Overall, the total unlocking speed for all tokens will decline by 43%, from approximately 5.1 million tokens per day to about 2.9 million WLD.

data.title: Bittensor Experiences Significant Market Value Loss Following Covenant AI Departure
data.description: Bittensor's market value dropped by nearly $900 million after Covenant AI announced its departure from the network. According to NS3.AI, CryptoSlate data revealed that TAO, Bittensor's native token, fell 27% from $338 to $285 within approximately two hours before slightly recovering to $294. CoinGlass data indicated that the event led to $11 million in long liquidations. The split has highlighted a governance dispute between Covenant AI founder Sam Dare and Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves.

data.title: WLD tokens will see a reduction in their unlocking rate in July, with an overall decrease of 43%.
data.description: The official World blog announced that the WLD token unlocking rate will be automatically reduced by approximately 43% starting July 24, 2026. Currently, 4.9 billion WLD tokens (49% of the total supply) have been unlocked, with approximately 3.3 billion in circulation. Specifically, the unlocking rate for the World community will decrease from 3.2 million tokens per day to 1.6 million tokens per day, a 50% reduction; the unlocking rate for the team and investors will decrease from 1.9 million tokens per day to 1.3 million tokens per day, a 32% reduction. The overall unlocking amount will decrease from approximately 5.1 million tokens per day to approximately 2.9 million tokens per day. WLD uses a continuous linear unlocking mechanism, with no one-time large-scale unlocking (cliff). This rate reduction stems from established on-chain contract arrangements aimed at gradually reducing selling pressure.

data.title: Circle unveils its vision for an internet finance system: advancing cross-chain interoperability, liquidity coordination, and institutional-grade asset issuance.
data.description: Circle officially released its 2026 vision for the development of its internet finance system, proposing to focus on building cross-chain interoperability infrastructure, liquidity orchestration capabilities, and an institutional-grade asset issuance system to promote the efficient flow of value within a multi-chain ecosystem. Regarding cross-chain infrastructure, Circle stated that it will launch features such as "Fast Transfer" in the future, enabling second-level settlement without waiting for final confirmation from the source chain, thus improving capital utilization efficiency. In terms of asset expansion, CCTP plans to expand to more assets beyond USDC, including EURC, USYC, and cirBTC, and support the issuance and circulation of real-world assets (RWA) across multiple chains. Furthermore, Circle is advancing cross-chain process coordination, simplifying multi-chain operation processes through tools such as Bridge Kit, Deposit Kit, Fee Service, and Workflows, achieving "one-click" cross-chain interaction, reducing development complexity, and improving user experience.

data.title: Tom Lee: The market may have bottomed out; I suggest focusing on assets that have led the market since the start of the war.
data.description: Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee stated on the X platform that although strong skepticism remains in the market, increasing signs indicate that the market has bottomed out. In an environment of high uncertainty, investors should focus on assets that have outperformed since the Iranian conflict, with Ethereum-related assets leading the pack, followed by Bitcoin-related assets. Specifically, Tom Lee prioritizes Ethereum and related assets (such as ETHA and BMNR), while also favoring Bitcoin and its ETFs (such as IBIT). He previously emphasized that Ethereum has demonstrated relative strength during geopolitical conflicts, becoming a "bellwether" for market resilience. Overall, Tom Lee believes that cryptocurrencies have proven to be a "wartime store of value," and the market has gradually digested negative factors, providing a foundation for further rebound in risk assets. However, this depends on the continued stability of the macroeconomic and geopolitical situation.

data.title: States Consider Fuel Tax Cuts Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is leading states across the United States to contemplate temporary reductions in fuel taxes as a relief measure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative could potentially cost states millions of dollars. The move aims to alleviate the financial burden on consumers facing rising fuel prices due to the geopolitical tensions. However, the potential loss in tax revenue is a significant concern for state budgets already strained by other economic pressures. As discussions continue, states are weighing the benefits of immediate consumer relief against the long-term fiscal impacts.

data.title: Russia Bans Stanford University as 'Undesirable' Organization
data.description: Russia has officially designated Stanford University as an 'undesirable' organization, adding it to a growing list of foreign entities banned within the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move is part of a broader crackdown on foreign institutions and organizations deemed to pose a threat to national security. The designation effectively prohibits the university from operating within Russia and restricts any collaboration with Russian entities. This action follows similar bans on other international organizations, reflecting ongoing tensions between Russia and Western institutions.

data.title: Significant NOM Transfer to Centralized Exchange Amid Price Drop
data.description:
On April 10, a substantial transfer of NOM tokens was observed. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, an address group holding at least 59% of NOM's circulating supply moved 23% of the tokens, totaling 674 million NOM, to a centralized exchange within the past hour. This transfer coincided with a 25% decline in NOM's price today.

The address group is likely responsible for driving a sixfold increase in NOM's value over the past two weeks, from $0.0017 to $0.0126. On April 1, they withdrew 1.72 billion NOM from a centralized exchange using seven wallets, representing 59% of NOM's total circulating supply of 2.9 billion tokens.

data.title: Iran Allegedly Charges Fees for Passage Through Hormuz Strait
data.description: An Israeli intelligence official has claimed that Iran is imposing fees on vessels passing through the Hormuz Strait. According to Odaily, the strait is reportedly under the complete control of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, which determines which ships are allowed to pass and, more critically, which are not.
